scope_note: QA Archaeogeometry Orbit Classification cert [311]. Primary sources: Iverson, B. (1975-1996) Quantum Arithmetic vols 1-2 (QA-1, QA-2); Thom, A. (1962) The Megalithic Unit of Length, J. Royal Statistical Society A 125:243-251 DOI 10.2307/2982493; Thom, A. (1967) Megalithic Sites in Britain, Oxford University Press, ISBN 978-0-19-813148-8; Wildberger, N.J. (2005) Divine Proportions: Rational Trigonometry, Wild Egg Books, ISBN 978-0-9757492-0-8. QA mapping: the five Pythagorean triangles documented by Thom as megalithic construction templates each generate a QA base pair (b,e) via cert [310] squaring map: d=b+e, C=2de, F=d^2-e^2, G=d^2+e^2. The mod-9 T-orbit sub-family of (b,e) — identified by which canonical seed {(1,1), (2,1), (1,4)} appears in its 24-step orbit — places the triangle on the QA excitation ladder: Fibonacci sub-orbit (seed (1,1), |f|=1 for rank-1,2 generators), Lucas sub-orbit (seed (2,1), |f|=5), Third sub-orbit (seed (1,4), generators reach (1,4) under mod-9 T-step). Sub-orbit classification uses mod-9 canonical seed membership; individual state f(b,e)=b^2+b*e-e^2 may differ from seed norm when mod-9 wraps multiple unbounded orbits (e.g., (5,1): |f(5,1)|=29 reaches (1,4) at mod-9 so in Third orbit). Builds on cert [310] (QA Rational Surveying, primitivity criterion, exact spreads) and cert [178] (QA Megalithic, MY quantum, fathom preference). CLAIM: (C1) all 5 Thom construction Pythagorean triples are primitive and have Cosmos-family generators in both mod-9 and mod-24; follows from cert [310] C3 (primitive iff gcd(b,e)=1 AND b odd => Cosmos). (C2) Rank-1 (3-4-5) and rank-2 (5-12-13) both have generating (b,e) in the Fibonacci sub-orbit (|f|=1 ground state); they are the unique two primitive Pythagorean triples with G <= 13 and Fibonacci sub-orbit generators: G=5 -> (1,1), G=13 -> (1,2). (C3) The 5 construction triangles collectively span all 3 mod-9 Cosmos sub-orbits: 2/5 Fibonacci (rank 1,2), 1/5 Lucas (rank 4: 7-24-25 -> (1,3)), 2/5 Third (rank 3: 8-15-17 -> (3,1); rank 5: 12-35-37 -> (5,1)); complete excitation ladder coverage; minimum-energy sub-orbit contains the two most common triangles. (C4) Among 31 distinct integer-MY diameters in Thom 1962 (84 circles, range 4-55 MY), exactly 4 are expressible as primitive Pythagorean hypotenuse G=d^2+e^2 (Fermat sum-of-two-squares): {5,13,17,29}; all 4 are Cosmos; G={5,13} have Fibonacci generators; G={17,29} have Third generators; zero have Lucas generators. (C5) Theorem NT: physical diameter in feet is float (observer projection); integer-MY diameter is QA-layer measurement; orbit_fam is discrete QA classification; Wildberger spread F^2/G^2 for each triangle is exact Fraction in (0,1) with s_F + s_C = 1 (cert [310] C4). Lineage: derived 2026-06-03 from Thom primary sources + QA orbit analysis.
